desc: Fix two critical security gaps in Africa Watch auth-db.js. CHANGE 1 — Remove JWT secret fallback: Line 14 currently reads `const JWT_SECRET = process.env.JWT_SECRET || 'aw-dev-secret-change-in-prod';`. Change this to throw a startup error if JWT_SECRET is not set: `if (!process.env.JWT_SECRET) { console.error('[FATAL] JWT_SECRET env var is not set. Set it in .env before starting the server.'); process.exit(1); } const JWT_SECRET = process.env.JWT_SECRET;`. CHANGE 2 — Replace hardcoded default admin password with a randomly generated one: Lines 162-171 currently seed admin with password 'AfricaWatch2024!'. Replace with: generate a random password using `crypto.randomBytes(12).toString('base64url')`, use that as the password, and print it clearly to the console ONCE (it will only appear when the DB is first initialised). Also add a `password_must_change` column to the users table (INTEGER DEFAULT 0) and set it to 1 for the seeded admin. Add a middleware function `requirePasswordChange` that checks if `req.user.password_must_change === 1` and returns 403 with message 'Password change required — use POST /auth/change-password' for all routes except /auth/change-password and /auth/logout. Add a POST /auth/change-password route that accepts { currentPassword, newPassword } (min 12 chars), verifies current, updates hash+salt, sets password_must_change=0. Export requirePasswordChange alongside requireAuth and requireRole.
stack: Node.js, better-sqlite3, jsonwebtoken, crypto — auth-db.js only
instructions: Return a valid unified git diff for a/output/auth-db.js only. No markdown fences. The diff must be directly applicable with `git apply`. Include all three changes: JWT_SECRET guard, random admin password, password_must_change flow with /auth/change-password route and requirePasswordChange middleware.
